tasks_completed.description: Registered test-writer in three harness sites
tasks_completed.changes: CP_AGENTS set + agent_types list + commands/dev.md for-loop wordlist (line 133-136) all gained 'test-writer'. Symmetric registration prevents one-of-three-broken consumability failures.
tasks_completed.rationale: Codex finding #3 — tool-policy alone is insufficient for consumability; the harness must also dispatch + checkpoint-track test-writer

tasks_completed.description: Made agents/test-writer.md procedure + Output JSON internally consistent with per-task scheme
tasks_completed.changes: Procedure step 5 rewritten to author per-task active manifest tests/generated/<task_id>/manifest.json AND upsert global index {kind:"index", tasks:[...]} at tests/generated/manifest.json. Output JSON Report manifest_path now per-task; added global_index_path + global_index_kind fields; preamble explicitly notes kind: index for global file. UPDATE-vs-CREATE Logic section reworded to point at per-task manifest (codex finding #3 follow-on).
tasks_completed.rationale: Codex finding #4 — internal contradiction between schema (per-task) and procedure (global) would have broken first dispatch

tasks_completed.description: Fixed qa.md Phase 5 + manifest_verification schema to per-task scheme
tasks_completed.changes: Phase 5 step 1 reads per-task manifest (venv-wrapped importlib); step 2 pytest tests/generated/<task_id>/ -q (scoped, not recursive); sentinel paragraph compressed to fit 200-char regex window between 'test_writer_expected' and 'tests/generated/manifest.json is missing'; preserved the legitimate global-index PRESENCE sentinel verbatim. Schema manifest_path is per-task; added global_index_path + global_index_kind: 'index' fields.
tasks_completed.rationale: Codex finding #5 — pytest scope was recursive (would re-collect sibling tasks) and reads pointed at global

tasks_completed.description: commands/dev.md Step 11 QA dispatch + Dev-handoff per-task manifest path
tasks_completed.changes: Step 11 QA dispatch prompt body now carries 'Per-task active test manifest: tests/generated/<task_id>/manifest.json' line. Dev-handoff (line ~666) 'Generated tests (when test-writer ran):' line now references per-task path; global file referenced only via annotated 'tests/generated/manifest.json (index)' form. Test-writer dispatch input block (line 633) updated per codex iter-2 to list both per-task manifest and global index; post-dispatch verification line (637) now requires all three artifacts: per-task manifest, global index, report.
tasks_completed.rationale: Codex finding #5 + iter-2 finding #2 — Dev and QA must receive the per-task path; orchestrator post-dispatch verification must check both manifest forms

tasks_completed.changes: Added /agent-scores.json, /agent-scores.json.lock, /.claude/specs/spec-*/ at end of file under explanatory header. Pre-existing 'specs/spec-*/' rule is relative, did NOT match .claude/specs/...

tasks_completed.description: Removed tracked telemetry artifacts from git index (preserved working tree)
tasks_completed.changes: 49 tracked artifacts removed from git index. -r flag REQUIRED because .claude/specs/spec-20260518-225715/ is a directory. Working-tree files preserved (.cached flag semantics). post-condition: git ls-files for the three targets returns empty + working-tree copies exist.
tasks_completed.rationale: Codex finding #6 — .gitignore is a no-op for already-tracked paths. User authorization via in-session Cycle-2 dispatch + spec Section 7 P2.3 explicit cleanup mandate

tasks_completed.description: Removed all agent-scores entries from README.md + INDEX.md
tasks_completed.changes: README.md:16-17 — removed two leaked listing entries. INDEX.md:3475-3476, 11519-11520, 21634-21635 — removed three pairs of leaked listing entries (6 lines total). INDEX.md:3863, 13659 — reworded score-update.sh description lines to drop the ~/.claude/agent-scores.json substring (replaced with 'agent score state file'). Final grep count agent-scores across all three files: 0.
tasks_completed.rationale: Spec Section 7 P2.4 — agent-scores files now gitignored, must drop from auto-generated indices

tasks_completed.description: Updated doc-sync SKIP lists + added path-aware specs filter
tasks_completed.changes: regen_readme.py SKIP_NAMES + tree.py SKIP_FILES gained 'agent-scores.json' and 'agent-scores.json.lock' (AST-verifiable via ast.literal_eval). tree.py also gained _is_dot_claude_specs(item) PATH-AWARE predicate matching name 'specs' AND parent '.claude'; integrated into the items filter. A blanket i.name == 'specs' check would have failed the AC-09 negative control (legitimate ordinary/specs/keep.txt must survive). scripts/score-update.sh:2 Description shebang comment reworded to drop ~/.claude/agent-scores.json substring (source-of-truth cut so doc-sync extract chain does not re-leak the path).
tasks_completed.rationale: Codex finding #7 — doc-sync filesystem scan does not consult .gitignore; without skip list update the next regen re-leaks. Path-aware predicate prevents suppression of legitimate non-.claude specs/ subtrees.

tasks_completed.description: Wrapped all 5 python3 invocations in same-line venv subshells
tasks_completed.changes: scripts/score-update.sh:71 and scripts/score-inject.sh:43 — heredoc forms wrapped as ( source ~/.claude/venv/bin/activate && python3 - args <<'PYEOF' ... PYEOF ). Closing ) added after the heredoc terminator. scripts/canary-verify.sh lines 102, 116, 137 — pipe forms wrapped as: echo X | ( source ~/.claude/venv/bin/activate && python3 Y ).
tasks_completed.rationale: Spec Section 7 P3.6 — venv must be sourced in same execution context that runs python3, else VIRTUAL_ENV is not inherited

tasks_completed.description: Eliminated Sub-step 12.x decimal labels; integer-step prose replacement
tasks_completed.changes: Line 822 — '**Sub-step 12.0 (apply spec section updates BEFORE decision tree)**' heading replaced with prose paragraph naming the eliminated decimal in narrative form (no literal Sub-step 12.0 token, just 'decimal sub-step'). Line 844 — cross-reference rewritten from 'Sub-step 12.0' to 'the spec section updates pass'. Line 861 — '**Sub-step 12.1: Mascot score-update**' heading replaced with prose paragraph. Line 1115 (Mascot Score Changes section) — cross-reference rewritten from 'Sub-step 12.1' to 'Step 12 (the score-update events pass, formerly labelled with a decimal sub-step ...)'. Regex 'Sub-step 12\.\d' file-wide match count: 0.
tasks_completed.rationale: Codex finding #8 — Sub-step 12.0 was a separate decimal violation. Both eliminated; procedural ordering preserved by keeping the work inside Step 12.

tasks_completed.description: Replaced /tmp/ literals with mktemp in canary-verify.sh
tasks_completed.changes: Line 79 /tmp/canary-safe.txt → safe_file=$(mktemp -t canary-safe.XXXXXX) + JSON payload referencing ${safe_file} + cleanup rm -f. Line 103 /tmp/canary-oversized-$$.txt → oversized=$(mktemp -t canary-oversized.XXXXXX). Surrounding comments also rephrased so no /tmp/ literal remains anywhere. grep '/tmp/' file: exit 1 (zero matches).
tasks_completed.rationale: Codex finding #9 — /tmp/canary-safe.txt was a second hardcoded literal (sibling to the spec-cited /tmp/canary-oversized-$$.txt); parameterizing both prevents TMPDIR-divergence breakage and Standard 9 violations

tasks_completed.description: Added Standard 6 Cycle-2 exemption paragraph with precise class enumeration
tasks_completed.changes: agents/style-inspector.md:322 — new single-paragraph exemption anchored '**Exemption (Cycle-2 user-facing source-language prompt strings — spec-20260518-225715 §5.1)**' enumerating four classes: (i) five rank labels 见习学徒/初级工匠/熟练工匠/资深工匠/宗师级 emitted by score-{inject,update}.sh; (ii) user-rating tail-phrase block; (iii) AskUserQuestion choice ladder including 5★/4★/3★/2★/1★ labels and 跳过 skip choice (broadened per codex iter-2 finding #4); (iv) score-inject neutral-header fallback. Re-affirmation clause: code comments AND stderr/diagnostic strings remain English-only across ALL scripts and agents (preserves Standard 6 protective intent). scripts/score-update.sh:24-27 usage help translated to English with rank-1..rank-5 label mapping + note explaining that the bound CJK rank labels still flow through stdout per the exemption. scripts/score-inject.sh:33-37 mid-tier neutral header comment translated to English; the CJK label inside the cat heredoc remains (bound user-facing prompt-output per spec §5.1).
tasks_completed.rationale: Codex finding #10 + iter-2 finding #4 — the original exemption only enumerated 跳过, leaving the five rating labels at risk of Standard 6 violation. Exemption now covers the full AskUserQuestion ladder. Diagnostic-text translations align actual code with the 'stderr remains English-only' clause in the exemption.

tasks_completed.changes: ALLOWED_AGENTS tuple gained 'test-writer' with explanatory comment. Without this, spec-check.py check-in/mark/waive --agent test-writer would reject with 'unknown agent' even though pretool-cp-checkin.py CP_AGENTS accepts test-writer reads — asymmetric registration would break the cp-state lifecycle.
tasks_completed.rationale: Codex consultation finding #1 — symmetry requirement between CP_AGENTS (PreToolUse Read handler) and ALLOWED_AGENTS (cp-state writer). Both must accept test-writer or the agent's checkpoint flow 404s.

tasks_completed.description: Spec file Section 2 + Section 3 Cycle 2 narrative
tasks_completed.changes: Section 2 — appended new ### Cycle 2 subsection with 13-item approach narrative (Cycle 1 content untouched). Section 3 — appended new ### Cycle 2 subsection with per-file line-level change log keyed by AC ID.
tasks_completed.rationale: agents/dev.md Overnight Spec Integration § After Implementation contract

git_rationale.root_cause_commit: d988d4a chore(docs): ship dev cycle 20260519-175339 + re-enable docs/dev (Cycle 1 delivery)
git_rationale.why_issue_occurred: Cycle 1's ACs were structurally scoped (verify Section 5 component existence) but not consumability/cleanliness/style-scoped. /close gate operates on the broader criteria; without pre-encoded ACs covering them, QA could PASS while /close failed.
git_rationale.how_fix_addresses_root: Cycle 2 implements the 9 Section 7 P1+P2+P3 items + 5 codex-supplied completeness extensions; each is now encoded as one of the 13 strict deterministic ACs that the /close gate's three failure axes (consumability, cleanliness, style) directly map onto.

diff_stats.justification_for_overage: 16 distinct files each requiring a small surgical edit + spec file Section 2/3 narrative (~53 lines) + per-file change log Section 3 (~50 lines). Each individual edit is at or near minimum; the line-count is driven by the breadth of the 9-item remediation, not depth at any single site. New _is_dot_claude_specs predicate is 1 function (3 LoC) — adding it as a separate def is the smallest path-aware predicate that satisfies AC-09's negative-control requirement.

dev.qa_notes: All 13 strict ACs verified PASS via the same regex/AST/runtime checks specified in the AC JSON. Build verification: bash -n on all 3 modified scripts, py_compile on all 5 modified Python files, json.load on tool-policy.v1.json — all OK. Behavioural smoke: bash scripts/score-inject.sh --agent dev produces expected rank-line + tail-phrase output. Codex adversarial consultation invoked (codex_required: true); 5 findings returned, 4 applied surgically (findings #1, #2, #3, #4), 1 documented as observation (finding #5: pre-existing working-tree drift outside this cycle's scope — see codex_consult.feedback_incorporated below).

self_verification.notes: All 13 strict ACs PASS via deterministic checks. Build: bash -n on 3 scripts, py_compile on 5 .py files, json.load on tool-policy.v1.json. Smoke: scripts/score-inject.sh --agent dev produces expected output with exit 0. AC-09 runtime fixture executed live (positive leak suppressed, negative control preserved, control file present).

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex (gpt-5.5 xhigh) returned 5 findings, each with PROPOSED_FIX. (#1) test-writer missing from scripts/spec-check.py ALLOWED_AGENTS — applied. (#2) commands/dev.md:633/637 test-writer dispatch still cites global manifest as 'Manifest:' and verifies only global — applied (now lists both per-task + global (index) and verifies all three artifacts). (#3) agents/test-writer.md UPDATE/CREATE Logic section still references global manifest — applied (now per-task). (#4) Standard 6 exemption only enumerates 跳过 but commands/close.md has 5 additional rating labels; also score-inject.sh:33 + score-update.sh:25-26 have Chinese in code comment / stderr help — applied: broadened exemption to cover the full AskUserQuestion choice ladder including 5★/4★/3★/2★/1★ labels; translated score-update.sh usage help + score-inject.sh comment to English so the diagnostics-remain-English-only clause is truthful. (#5) Working tree includes out-of-scope changes (settings.json effortLevel, agents/changelog-analyst.md /commit env-var) — DOCUMENTED as observation: these are NOT in my Cycle 2 file_to_modify list and were present in the working tree before this cycle started (per orchestrator dispatch's 'Working-tree state notes: 327+ uncommitted files from prior cycles + parallel sessions'). I did not touch them and they are out of scope for Cycle 2's diff.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Findings #1, #2, #3, #4 applied as surgical edits (scripts/spec-check.py +5 LoC; commands/dev.md test-writer dispatch +2 LoC; agents/test-writer.md UPDATE/CREATE section reworded; agents/style-inspector.md exemption broadened to cover full rating ladder; scripts/score-update.sh + scripts/score-inject.sh diagnostic-text translated). All 13 ACs re-validated PASS after the codex-driven changes. Finding #5 documented in out_of_scope_observations and flagged to orchestrator below.

findings.reason: Working-tree drift in settings.json and agents/changelog-analyst.md was present BEFORE Cycle 2 started per orchestrator dispatch notes. These files are NOT in my files_to_modify per BA context. I did not touch them. /close gate's introduced_in_diff scoping will correctly exclude them from Cycle-2 inspector findings if QA scopes properly to commits since d988d4a.

out_of_scope_observations.observation: Working tree contains 327+ uncommitted files from prior cycles + parallel sessions (per orchestrator dispatch notes). My Cycle 2 diff touches only the 17 files listed in dev.files_modified. The parallel-session changes to settings.json (effortLevel xhigh→high) and agents/changelog-analyst.md (/commit env-var) are NOT in my scope. Recommendation: /close cleanliness inspector should scope introduced_in_diff to commits since the Cycle 1 delivery commit d988d4a, or to the specific 17-file commit produced from this cycle.

recommendations: Commit Cycle 2's 17 files + 1 git index state change as a single focused commit so /close cleanliness inspector can scope to the Cycle-2 introduced_in_diff cleanly.

Cycle 3 should pick up the 5 deferred P4 items (B-IT3F-1 BA tool-policy prefix patch, B-IT3F-4 session-info stdout migration, blast-radius Phase 2 untracked-file detection, QA Phase 5 trigger asymmetry, canary fail-closed depth) per spec Section 7 P4.

Future cycles invoking /commit should include scripts/spec-check.py in the cycle's cleanliness-inspector scope so the ALLOWED_AGENTS list stays symmetric with CP_AGENTS and agent_types as new agents are added.
